summary: SGD-style noisy updates descend from stochastic approximation.
Momentum SGD adds a velocity state to gradient descent/SGD.
Momentum buffer stores an exponentially decayed update direction.
AdaGrad adapts SGD steps with accumulated squared gradients.
AdaGrad uses per-coordinate accumulated-gradient scaling.
RMSProp adds forgetting/EMA to AdaGrad-style squared-gradient scaling.
RMSProp uses an EMA of squared gradients.
Adam combines first-moment momentum with second-moment adaptive scaling.
Adam builds on RMSProp-style second-moment scaling with bias correction.
Adam tracks a second raw moment with EMA and bias correction.
AdamW decouples weight decay from Adam adaptive updates.
AdamW applies decoupled weight decay.
Shampoo maintains structured preconditioner matrices over tensor axes.
SOAP improves and stabilizes Shampoo with Adam-style moments in the preconditioner eigenbasis.
SOAP runs Adam-like second moments in the Shampoo eigenbasis.
Muon applies matrix orthogonalization after momentum for 2D hidden-layer updates.
Muon can be interpreted as an accumulation-free Shampoo-like orthogonalized update.
Muon orthogonalizes each 2D update matrix before applying it.
Muon uses a tuned Newton-Schulz polynomial iteration for fast approximate orthogonalization.
Muon papers commonly use AdamW as the main LLM training baseline.
Orthogonalized matrix updates correspond to UV^T/polar-factor directions.
Spectral-norm trust-region theory explains orthogonalized gradient directions.
Muon efficiency work studies larger critical batch sizes and compute-time tradeoffs.

Dion targets orthonormalized update costs exposed by Muon under sharding.
AdaMuon combines Muon orthogonal updates with elementwise adaptivity.
AdaMuon adds second-moment adaptive scaling to orthogonal update directions.
MuonBP changes Muon orthogonalization scheduling for model-parallel throughput.
MuonBP is built around block-periodic orthogonalization.
FedMuon applies Muon-style local updates in federated learning.
TEON generalizes Muon from layer-wise matrices to structured higher-order tensors.
Newton-Muon adds right preconditioning from a Newton-style surrogate model.
Muon^2 inserts adaptive second-moment preconditioning before Muon orthogonalization.
Muon^2 borrows Adam-style second-moment preconditioning.
MuonQ compresses Muon optimizer state to low precision.
MuonQ is a low-bit optimizer-state quantization framework.
MONA adds Nesterov-like acceleration to Muon.
LoRA-Muon adapts Muon spectral steepest descent to low-rank adapters.
LoRA-Muon is formulated on the low-rank manifold.
Scales Muon with weight decay and update-scale adjustments for LLM training.
Reports scaling-law and efficiency comparisons against AdamW.
Analyzes practical compute-time efficiency and batch-size behavior of Muon.
Polar Express proposes an improved matrix sign/polar iteration for Muon-like use.
Shows 8-bit Muon can reduce optimizer-state footprint while maintaining performance in studied settings.
Analyzes Muon in ViT recipes and gradient spectra.
Studies Muon under adversarial training and spectral stability.
